about.title: About Me
about.content: I am Shreya Reddy, an AI/ML Engineer with 4+ years developing, shipping, and supporting RAG-based agents and LLM-powered applications for non-technical enterprise users across healthcare and fintech. I specialize in prompt engineering, document ingestion pipelines, chunking strategy, answer quality validation, and cross-functional AI enablement using OpenAI API, LangChain, and LlamaIndex. I also have deep experience in end-to-end MLOps, real-time feature engineering, and multi-model ensemble deployment on AWS SageMaker and Google Cloud Vertex AI. I have a track record of translating ambiguous operational problems into production-ready AI tools with measurable adoption impact, including 30–50% efficiency gains and multi-million-dollar revenue impact through scalable ML infrastructure, GenAI agents, and regulatory-compliant model governance.

skills.title: Skills & Expertise
skills.content: I have a diverse skill set spanning ML/AI, data engineering, cloud platforms, MLOps, and full-stack development.

education.title: Education
education.content: I hold a Master of Science in Data Analytics Engineering from George Mason University, Fairfax, VA (Aug 2023 – May 2025) and a Bachelor of Technology in Information Technology from Jawaharlal Nehru Technological University, Hyderabad, India (Aug 2019 – Jun 2023).

fun.title: Fun & Hobbies
fun.content: In my free time, I love going for walks and runs — it's my way of clearing my head and staying energized. I'm also a big café explorer! Discovering new coffee spots and trying different brews is something I genuinely look forward to. Doodling is my favorite hobby — I can lose track of time just sketching random things. And when I'm at home, I love organizing and styling my space to make it feel just right, because a well-arranged space genuinely lifts my mood and helps me feel my best.

experience.title: Professional Experience
experience.content: I have experience building production ML systems across healthcare, fintech, and telecom, contributing to both AI/ML engineering and data engineering roles.

highlights: Built and maintained RAG-based AI agents using OpenAI API (GPT-4), LangChain, LangGraph, and Pinecone on AWS, implementing document ingestion pipelines, chunking strategies, vector embeddings, and prompt template libraries, cutting manual policy lookup time by 65% for supply chain teams

Validated answer quality and tuned multi-agent behavior through structured testing of retrieval accuracy, prompt engineering configurations, and chunking parameters, iterating on agent design to improve response reliability for non-technical end users

Designed and delivered AI enablement programs including onboarding guides, user documentation, and training materials for 150+ cross-functional stakeholders across procurement, operations, and finance
Deployed AI agent endpoints on AWS with REST APIs and automated retraining pipelines, tracking RAG pipeline performance and adoption metrics across enterprise integrations
Migrated pharmaceutical supply chain ML pipelines from Databricks to Google Cloud Vertex AI, rebuilding drug shortage risk scoring using XGBoost and LSTM ensemble for scalable production deployment

Designed real-time feature pipelines on Pub/Sub and Kafka ingesting EHR events, supplier fill-rate streams, and external signals into BigQuery, replacing overnight batch processing with sub 3-minute feature latency

Developed a three-model payment decisioning system on AWS SageMaker for card application scoring, limit optimization, and collections propensity using XGBoost, LightGBM, and Gradient Boosting, processing 2M+ monthly applications and improving credit approval accuracy by 18%
Engineered a centralized feature store on SageMaker Feature Store with signals including payment ratio, utilization trend, and delinquency history across batch and real-time scoring workloads

Wrote clear use-case documentation for model outputs and SHAP-based adverse action reasons, translating complex ML decisions into plain language for non-technical Risk and Compliance teams under ECOA and Regulation B

Maintained model monitoring, drift detection, and automated retraining using MLflow and SageMaker Pipelines with CI/CD via GitHub Actions, tracking performance and adoption metrics across weekly production deployments

Built ETL pipelines using AWS Glue and PySpark to migrate 800GB+ of telecom customer usage and billing data from on-prem Oracle systems into Amazon S3, supporting downstream analytics for business intelligence teams
Designed Redshift data warehouse tables and optimized SQL transformation scripts to support incremental loads, enabling business intelligence teams to run churn and revenue analytics
Automated pipeline scheduling using Apache Airflow across S3, Glue, and Redshift, handling failure alerts and retry logic to achieve 99.7% SLA compliance for client reporting

projects.title: Projects
projects.content: My projects span AI-powered portfolio applications, production ML systems in healthcare and fintech, and large-scale data engineering pipelines in telecom.

items.name: AI Portfolio — This Website
highlights: Built a fully interactive AI-powered personal portfolio where an LLM answers questions about my background, skills, and experience in real time
Implemented a RAG pipeline using Mistral embeddings and vector similarity search over a structured knowledge base to ground AI responses in accurate personal data
Designed a multi-section chat UI with section-specific AI responses for About, Experience, Projects, Skills, Education, and Contact
Engineered a Node.js/Express backend with an ingest pipeline that indexes knowledge into an in-memory vector store for semantic retrieval
Built with React, TailwindCSS, and Framer Motion for smooth animated UI including expandable project cards and a fluid canvas background

items.name: Pharmaceutical Supply Chain ML System — Cardinal Health
highlights: Migrated pharmaceutical supply chain ML pipelines from Databricks to Google Cloud Vertex AI, rebuilding drug shortage risk scoring using an XGBoost and LSTM ensemble
Designed real-time feature pipelines on Pub/Sub and Kafka ingesting EHR events, supplier fill-rate streams, and external signals into BigQuery with sub 3-minute feature latency
Built an internal RAG-based procurement assistant using LangChain and Pinecone, enabling supply chain teams to query drug shortage policies and inventory reports in natural language
Deployed risk score endpoints via Cloud Run through an Apigee-secured REST API with automated retraining on Vertex AI Pipelines, consumed by InteLogix dashboards and SAP ERP systems

items.name: Credit Risk Scoring System — Discover Financial Services
highlights: Developed a three-model credit risk scoring system on AWS SageMaker for card application decisioning, limit optimization, and collections propensity
Engineered a centralized feature store on SageMaker Feature Store with payment ratio, utilization trend, and delinquency history signals across batch and real-time workloads
Integrated SHAP explainability via SageMaker Clarify to generate adverse action reasons for declined applications, meeting ECOA and Regulation B compliance requirements
Maintained automated retraining and drift monitoring using SageMaker Pipelines and MLflow across weekly production deployments

items.name: Telecom Data Engineering Pipeline — Tech Mahindra
highlights: Built batch ingestion pipelines using AWS Glue and PySpark to migrate telecom customer usage and billing data from on-prem Oracle systems into Amazon S3
Designed Redshift data warehouse tables and optimized SQL transformation scripts to support incremental loads for churn and revenue analytics
Automated daily pipeline scheduling using Apache Airflow across S3, Glue, and Redshift with failure alerts and retry logic to maintain data freshness for client SLAs

availability.title: Job Availability & Career
availability.content: I am currently open to new opportunities in AI/ML Engineering, MLOps, and Data Engineering. I am actively looking for full-time roles where I can contribute to production ML systems, GenAI applications, and scalable data infrastructure. My ideal role involves LLM-powered pipelines, cloud ML platforms like AWS SageMaker or Google Cloud Vertex AI, and real-world impact across industries like healthcare, fintech, or tech. I completed my Master of Science in Data Analytics Engineering from George Mason University in May 2025 and am based in the US with full work authorization. I can start immediately or within a short notice period depending on the role.
availability.careerGap: There is no significant experience gap in my career. After completing my role at Tech Mahindra in July 2023, I transitioned directly into my Master's program at George Mason University starting August 2023. During graduate school, I also gained industry experience — I worked as a Machine Learning Engineer at Discover Financial Services from May 2024 to June 2025, and joined Cardinal Health as an AI/ML Engineer in June 2025. So my graduate studies and professional work overlapped, and I have been continuously building production ML skills throughout.

contact.title: Contact
contact.content: You can reach me directly via email or phone, and explore my work on LinkedIn and GitHub.

items.name: OpenAI Models (GPT-4o, GPT-4, GPT-3.5 Turbo)
items.content: I have hands-on experience with OpenAI's GPT model family across multiple production and personal projects. I use GPT-4o and GPT-4 for complex reasoning tasks, multi-step agent workflows, and high-quality text generation in RAG pipelines. GPT-3.5 Turbo is my go-to for cost-efficient chat completions where latency matters. I work with the OpenAI API via both direct REST calls and through LangChain integrations, using features like function calling and tool use to build structured AI agents. I've used OpenAI embeddings (text-embedding-3-small and text-embedding-ada-002) for semantic search and vector similarity in RAG systems, storing them in Pinecone and FAISS. In my procurement assistant at Cardinal Health, I evaluated GPT-4 for document Q&A over drug shortage policies before settling on the final stack.

items.name: Anthropic Claude Models (Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Claude 3 Opus, Claude 3 Haiku)
items.content: I have worked with Anthropic's Claude model family and appreciate its strengths in long-context understanding, instruction-following, and safe, nuanced responses. Claude 3.5 Sonnet is my preferred model for tasks requiring deep reasoning and well-structured outputs — I've used it for code generation, document summarization, and complex multi-turn conversations. Claude 3 Opus handles the most demanding reasoning and analysis tasks where quality is the top priority. Claude 3 Haiku is excellent for fast, low-latency inference in production pipelines where cost efficiency is important. I interact with Claude via the Anthropic SDK and REST API, using system prompts and tool use to build agentic workflows. I've also experimented with Claude's large context window (up to 200K tokens) for ingesting full documents and codebases in a single prompt.

items.name: Google Gemini Models (Gemini 1.5 Pro, Gemini 1.5 Flash, Gemini 2.0 Flash)
items.content: I have experience with Google's Gemini model family, particularly in the context of my work on Google Cloud Vertex AI at Cardinal Health. Gemini 1.5 Pro is a powerful model I've evaluated for multimodal tasks and long-context document understanding — its 1M token context window is impressive for processing large structured datasets. Gemini 1.5 Flash and Gemini 2.0 Flash are fast, cost-efficient models I've used for summarization and classification tasks within Vertex AI Pipelines. I access Gemini models both through the Vertex AI SDK for enterprise deployments and through the Gemini API (Google AI Studio) for prototyping. I've integrated Gemini into LangChain-based pipelines using the ChatGoogleGenerativeAI wrapper, and explored its native tool use and function calling capabilities for building agents on Google Cloud.

items.name: Mistral AI Models (Mistral Large, Mistral Small, Mistral Embeddings)
items.content: I actively use Mistral AI models in production, including in this portfolio itself. Mistral Small powers the chat responses on this website, and Mistral Embeddings (mistral-embed) handles the semantic vector search over my knowledge base. I chose Mistral for this project because of its strong performance-to-cost ratio and excellent API reliability. Mistral Large is a capable model I've used for complex instruction-following and structured output generation. I've worked with the Mistral API directly (REST and streaming) and through LangChain integrations. The streaming API is particularly well-suited for real-time chat applications where perceived latency matters, which is why I implemented SSE-based streaming in this portfolio.
